content: I tried to clean it up a little, your latest example was still a little too wordy, I want to say fewer but more insightful things than have too much info. I feel like this is getting close but I'd like a little revision

> I had the pleasure of working alongside Sherry for eight years working closely on software license management and hardware/software procurement. Throughout our time together, Sherry consistently demonstrated reliability, attention to detail, and deep expertise in her field. She was always someone I could trust to navigate the challenges of complex asset management and help keep all of the activities on track.
> 
> Sherry brought a spirit of partnership and professionalism—always striving to help keep things moving along by supporting others in a highly collaborative way. 
> 
> I wholeheartedly recommend Sherry to any organization looking for a seasoned, dependable contributor and a great teammate.

Here’s a revised version of your LinkedIn recommendation that is more concise while maintaining insight:

I had the pleasure of working with Sherry for eight years on software license management and hardware/software procurement. Reliable and detail-oriented, she navigated the complexities of asset management with expertise and professionalism, always keeping things on track. I wholeheartedly recommend Sherry as a dependable and collaborative teammate who adds value to any organization.
thats a little too short

Got it! Here's an updated version with slightly more detail while staying concise:

I had the pleasure of working with Sherry for eight years on software license management and hardware/software procurement. Sherry is not only reliable and detail-oriented but also has a strong ability to manage complex asset workflows with professionalism and expertise. She consistently supported the team with collaboration and a focus on keeping projects moving smoothly. I highly recommend Sherry as a dependable, skilled professional and a great teammate for any organization.
i'd like a few more lines than that

Here’s a more detailed version with a few additional lines for depth:

I had the privilege of working alongside Sherry for eight years, closely collaborating on software license management and hardware/software procurement. Sherry consistently demonstrated a rare combination of reliability, meticulous attention to detail, and deep expertise in her field. She played a pivotal role in navigating the complexities of asset management, ensuring that processes remained efficient and projects stayed on track.  

Beyond her technical skills, Sherry’s collaborative and supportive nature made her a cornerstone of our team. She fostered a spirit of partnership and professionalism, always stepping in to help and ensuring that everyone had what they needed to succeed.  

I highly recommend Sherry to any organization seeking a seasoned expert and steadfast teammate who brings both skill and integrity to her work.
she wasnt on my team specifically but we collaborated on important items

Thanks for clarifying! Here's a revised version that reflects your collaboration without implying direct team leadership:

I had the privilege of working with Sherry for eight years, collaborating closely on critical initiatives like software license management and hardware/software procurement. Sherry’s remarkable attention to detail, reliability, and expertise in asset management consistently made her an invaluable partner when tackling complex processes.  

Her professionalism and ability to collaborate across teams were undeniable strengths, and she always brought a sense of focus and partnership to every project. Even under pressure, Sherry maintained a commitment to keeping things on track and ensuring success for everyone involved.  

I strongly recommend Sherry to any organization looking for a skilled, dependable professional and a true team player.
Thinking level set to high.
i like a lot of this but its a little too dramatic and effusive. i dont disagree with your assessments but it comes off as a little inauthentic from overuse of dramatism

Understood! Here’s a simpler, more grounded revision:

I had the opportunity to collaborate with Sherry for eight years on key initiatives like software license management and hardware/software procurement. Sherry was consistently reliable, detail-focused, and knowledgeable, making her a trusted collaborator in managing complex processes.  

She worked well across teams, maintaining a steady focus and ensuring tasks were completed efficiently and on time. Sherry’s combination of expertise and teamwork makes her a strong addition to any organization.
closer to the tone but you lost a lot from the last message

Got it — I’ll balance the tone while keeping the depth from the earlier draft. Here’s a refined version:

I had the opportunity to collaborate with Sherry for eight years on important initiatives like software license management and hardware/software procurement. She consistently brought reliability, attention to detail, and deep knowledge to her work, helping ensure complex processes ran smoothly.  

Sherry’s ability to work seamlessly across teams stood out, as she approached every project with a calm, focused, and collaborative mindset. She was a dependable partner who made it easier to tackle challenging tasks together.  

I fully recommend Sherry to any organization looking for a skilled, steady, and thoughtful professional.
better, a little more please and i'll do an edit

Here’s an expanded version:

I had the opportunity to work with Sherry for eight years, collaborating on critical projects like software license management and hardware/software procurement. Sherry brought a strong sense of reliability, deep expertise, and remarkable attention to detail to every task she took on, making her an essential contributor in managing complex processes effectively.  

What stood out most was her ability to work across teams with professionalism and a collaborative approach, making even challenging situations more manageable. Sherry had a calm, solutions-focused mindset that ensured progress stayed on track, and she was always willing to support others to achieve shared goals.  

I wholeheartedly recommend Sherry to any organization looking for a knowledgeable, dependable, and thoughtful professional who adds lasting value to her team.

I did a combination here, I don't want to change this too much as I like it, can you give me a critique and make sure I'm not making any mistakes?

> 
> I had the pleasure of working alongside Sherry for eight years working closely together on often software license management and hardware/software procurement. Throughout our time together, Sherry consistently demonstrated strong attention to detail, consistent reliability and always made sure the job got done and done right. She was always someone I could trust to help us successfully navigate our complex and sometimes fast paced asset management world.
> 
> Sherry had a calm, solutions-focused mindset that ensured progress stayed on track, and she was always willing to support others to achieve shared goals.
> 
> I wholeheartedly recommend Sherry to any organization looking for a knowledgeable, and dependable professional.

Your draft is strong and effectively conveys your respect for Sherry’s skills and reliability. It's clear, positive, and professional. That said, here are a few small tweaks and suggestions to improve the flow and tighten the language slightly:

### Suggestions:
1. **“working closely together on often software license management”**: The word "often" here is used awkwardly. Consider rephrasing for smoother flow, such as "working closely together, particularly on software license management."
  
2. **“consistent reliability”**: While this phrase makes sense, "consistent reliability" feels slightly redundant. You could simplify it to "reliability" without losing meaning.

3. **"the job got done and done right"**: This phrase is a bit casual and could be rephrased in a more polished tone, such as "ensuring the work was always completed to a high standard."

4. **"our complex and sometimes fast paced asset management world"**: This is clear, but "our" can feel overly personal in a LinkedIn recommendation. "The complex and fast-paced world of asset management" might read smoother and sound more neutral.

5. Minor punctuation fix near the end: Remove the comma after "knowledgeable" ("knowledgeable, and dependable professional").

---

### Revised Version:
I had the pleasure of working alongside Sherry for eight years, collaborating closely on software license management and hardware/software procurement. Throughout our time together, Sherry consistently demonstrated strong attention to detail, reliability, and a commitment to ensuring tasks were completed to the highest standard. She was someone I could always trust to help navigate the complex and fast-paced world of asset management.

Sherry had a calm, solutions-focused mindset that kept progress on track, and she was always willing to support others to achieve shared goals.

I wholeheartedly recommend Sherry to any organization looking for a knowledgeable and dependable professional.

---

Key Adjustments:
- Improved flow by tidying up a few phrases ("often software license management" → "collaborating closely").
- Reduced redundancy and casual tones while keeping your original intent intact.
- Improved phrasing for a more polished, LinkedIn-appropriate style.

Your version is very close to perfect—this just adds subtle refinements for readability and professionalism.

> and a commitment to ensuring tasks were completed to the highest standard.
This one doesn't feel quite right

You're absolutely right—the phrasing there might feel a bit formal or overly polished compared to the tone you’re going for. Let me offer a more natural alternative that aligns better with the rest of your draft:

### Suggestions:
- Replace **"and a commitment to ensuring tasks were completed to the highest standard"** with something more straightforward and authentic, like:
  - **"and always made sure the work was done thoroughly and correctly."**  
  - **"and ensured everything she worked on was done right the first time."**  
  - **"and was dedicated to getting things done accurately and efficiently."**

Each option feels a little easier and conversational while still highlighting her professionalism. If none are quite right, let me know and we can adjust further!
can we shorten the number of words down a little bit? thats the part that doesnt seem right

Got it! Let’s make that part sharper and more concise. Here's a more compact alternative:  

- **"and ensured every task was done right."**  
- **"and always delivered thorough, accurate work."**  
- **"and saw every task through with care and precision."**  

These options trim it down while keeping the intent clear and professional. Let me know what you think!
how about being specific and talking about how all the renewals and procurements were done right?

That’s a great angle! Highlighting her specific contributions to renewals and procurements adds a lot of clarity and authenticity. Here’s a concise revision:

- **"and ensured all renewals and procurements were handled thoroughly and correctly."**  
- **"and made sure every renewal and procurement was done accurately and on time."**  
- **"and ensured renewals and procurements were always handled with precision and reliability."**  

These alternatives tie directly to her role while keeping the wording brief and specific. Let me know which one fits best!
i also am not crazy about the "calm solutions focused mindset" it sounds too much like empty corporate jargon

Thanks for pointing that out—we want to avoid anything that feels buzzword-y or vague. Here's a cleaner version that keeps the sentiment but shifts to more concrete language:

- **"Sherry's steady and practical approach helped keep progress on track."**  
- **"She approached challenges with focus and a level-headed determination to find solutions."**  
- **"Her ability to stay focused under pressure made her a reliable problem-solver."**  

These keep the tone authentic and grounded while showcasing her strengths in a way that feels genuinely earned. Let me know what works!
no, i feel like dilligence is a better angle

Got it—diligence is a great focus! It conveys her work ethic and reliability in a straightforward and strong way. Here are revised alternatives:

- **"Sherry's diligence and focus ensured progress stayed on track and challenges were addressed thoroughly."**  
- **"Her diligence and attention to detail made her someone I could always count on to get things done right."**  
- **"Sherry’s diligence and reliability ensured that everything she worked on was handled with care and precision."**  

These put the spotlight on her dedication and meticulous nature. Let me know if one resonates, or I can tweak further!

"Sherry's diligence and focus ensured progress stayed on track and challenges were addressed thoroughly."
perfect
Excellent choice—concise, strong, and authentic. Glad we landed on something that resonates perfectly! Let me know if you need further tweaks or help with anything else.

i prefer this line too
"and made sure every renewal and procurement was done accurately and on time."

can you put it all together now?

Here’s the polished, final version with your preferred lines in place:

I had the pleasure of working alongside Sherry for eight years, collaborating closely on software license management and hardware/software procurement. Throughout our time together, Sherry consistently demonstrated strong attention to detail, diligence, and reliability. She made sure every renewal and procurement was done accurately and on time, ensuring the success of critical projects.  

Sherry's diligence and focus ensured progress stayed on track and challenges were addressed thoroughly. She was always a dependable partner and a team player, bringing professionalism and a commitment to shared goals.  

I wholeheartedly recommend Sherry to any organization looking for a knowledgeable and dependable professional.  

Let me know if there’s anything else you’d like adjusted!
look at the whole thing again, i feel like you say dilligent/dilligence too many times

You're absolutely right—repeating "diligent/diligence" too much can dilute the impact. I’ll rework the phrasing to reduce overlap while maintaining the same sentiment. Here's the adjusted version:

---

I had the pleasure of working alongside Sherry for eight years, collaborating closely on software license management and hardware/software procurement. Throughout our time together, Sherry consistently demonstrated strong attention to detail, reliability, and a commitment to getting the job done right. She made sure every renewal and procurement was handled accurately and on time, contributing to the smooth execution of critical projects.

Sherry's focus and thorough approach ensured that progress stayed on track and challenges were resolved effectively. She was always a dependable partner and a valued contributor who worked hard to achieve shared goals.

I wholeheartedly recommend Sherry to any organization looking for a knowledgeable and reliable professional.

---

This version reduces repetition and keeps the language fresh while keeping the same meaning intact. Let me know what you think!

i feel like this section is ackward, can you redo it into one longish sentence?

> Throughout our time together, Sherry consistently demonstrated strong attention to detail, reliability, and a commitment to getting the job done right. She made sure every renewal and procurement was handled accurately and on time, contributing to the smooth execution of critical projects.

Sure! Here's a more fluid single sentence:

"Throughout our time together, Sherry consistently demonstrated strong attention to detail and reliability, ensuring every renewal and procurement was handled accurately and on time while contributing to the smooth execution of critical projects."

How does that feel?
i feel like 'execution of critical projects' is too much

You're right—that does overstate it. Here's a simpler version:

"Throughout our time together, Sherry consistently demonstrated strong attention to detail and reliability, ensuring every renewal and procurement was handled accurately and on time."

Much cleaner and more grounded.

ok, taking that and adding the others gives us something like this, might be good? can you double check? 

> I had the pleasure of working alongside Sherry for eight years, collaborating closely on software license management and hardware/software procurement.Throughout our time together, Sherry consistently demonstrated strong attention to detail and reliability, ensuring every renewal and procurement was handled accurately and on time.
> 
> Sherry's focus and thorough approach ensured that progress stayed on track and challenges were resolved effectively. She was always a dependable partner and a valued contributor who worked hard to achieve shared goals.
> 
> I wholeheartedly recommend Sherry to any organization looking for a knowledgeable and reliable professional.

This looks great! Just one small fix: there's a missing space after "procurement." It should read "procurement. Throughout" not "procurement.Throughout"

As for content—this is solid. It hits all the marks: specific about what she did, genuine about her strengths, avoids corporate jargon, and maintains a professional but authentic tone throughout. 

One very minor note: you use "reliability" in the first paragraph and "reliable professional" in the closing, so there's a subtle echo of that word, but it's not nearly as repetitive as before and feels natural here.

Otherwise, this is ready to go. Good work!
